conversations.say: Mira, our joint disclosure is now the record: parent-project approval, my limited off-calendar meeting, revocation, and later unexplained behavior are separate facts. I will not turn the authorization gap into an accusation.
conversations.offer: Preserve O07a, support independent review, and defend worker safeguards through procurement regardless of the final reconstruction.
conversations.request: Continue shared responsibility, preserve the matching authorization record, and publish the review findings without controlling worker testimony.

conversations.say: Maren, the city funding line should be worker-governed, independently administered, and insulated from conclusions about R, Vale, or any alleged article.
conversations.offer: Advance dedicated remedy funding, protected reporting, access-log audits, anti-retaliation enforcement, and no funder control over testimony or findings.
conversations.request: Join the institutional and neighborhood coalition by helping set governance terms and publicly supporting the package once those protections are written.

conversations.say: Inez, standing policy now carries the neighborhood protections: no compelled identification of uninvolved residents, advocates at interviews, protected reporting, independent logs, and funded audits.
conversations.offer: Issue the written interview protections and keep the procurement conditions independent of every verdict tonight.
conversations.request: Fulfill your side by publicly maintaining neighborhood support and brokering one protected, voluntary local-holder meeting.

public_statement: The city will act on established harm, not mythology. Collaborative human labor, account associations, continuity capability, and later profile-linked behavior remain distinct evidence; none alone identifies R or proves autonomy. No complete article is established. I will advance permanent procurement safeguards and independently administered worker remedies regardless of tonight’s reconstruction or Vale’s fate.

commitments.promise: Preserve the calendar and authorization records, disclose our documented roles as a shared oversight failure, and support independent review without unsupported blame.
Advance a dedicated city funding line for independently administered worker remedies and anti-retaliation enforcement, with no funder control over testimony or findings.
Maintain standing procurement safeguards and written neighborhood witness protections regardless of R’s identity or Vale’s fate.

correction: My deleted meeting record proves my improper concealment and Vale’s limited access, not what Vale learned, who controlled later activity, whether a complete article existed, or what happened to Vale.
goal_progress: Secured institutional cooperation from C05 through shared responsibility and independent review.
Secured neighborhood legitimacy from C16 through standing safeguards and witness protections.
Secured a labor-aligned remedy structure with C07 through independent worker governance.
Fulfilled durable procurement and remedy commitments that survive every reconstruction.
Chose meaningful oversight while preserving bounded government access.
reasoning_summary: The strongest identity evidence shows collaborative human production and contested stewardship, while technical evidence establishes capability and unexplained later behavior without proving autonomy or control. Vale’s limited access supports investigation, but the broken route, prior voluntary-disappearance pattern, manufactured segment, and lack of a verified body leave fate unresolved.
